topic: label-body alignment — close O6 from set-parity witness 2026-04-27 (assert each _assert_shape("CN rawNN", _check_rawMM())'s NN == MM, catching mislabeled lines that count-parity preserves)
predecessor_obstruction_closed: O6 (label-body alignment NOT checked — _assert_shape("C5 raw75", _check_raw87()) would silently pass parity but mislabel)

alpha_absorbed.payload_summary: predecessor cycle O6 — count parity is sufficient for forgot-to-mirror but blind to within-line label/body misalignment

gamma_bridge.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_gw (offline-fallback; cycle is local source-edit + selftest exec + live negative-path verification)

falsifiers_preregistered.condition: label-body helper invocation hypothesis — _cmd_selftest invokes _selftest_check_label_body() after _selftest_check_parity()
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: exec selftest; grep 'PASS label-body' on stdout
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: stdout contains 'PASS label-body' line → PASS; missing → FAIL

falsifiers_preregistered.condition: alignment count hypothesis — all 10 _assert_shape lines parsed and label NN == body NN
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: exec selftest; parse 'PASS label-body — N lines aligned' line

falsifiers_preregistered.condition: negative-path live verification hypothesis — when one line is intentionally mislabeled, helper exits 5 with line-number + label/body diagnostic
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: edit _assert_shape("C5 raw75", _check_raw75()) to _assert_shape("C5 raw75", _check_raw71()); run selftest; observe rc + stderr; revert
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: live observed: rc=5 + 'selftest FAIL label-body — line N label=75 body=71' eprintln → PASS; revert clean → original PASS state restored

falsifiers_preregistered.condition: no regression hypothesis — non-selftest --report --target-rule raw:97 still emits fail_count=0, checks_total=10, verdict=ok
string-API-restraint hypothesis — helper uses only single-arg index_of (no 2-arg form) to stay within established hexa stdlib usage
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: static read of helper body — count .index_of( call sites and verify each has only one argument
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: all index_of calls in helper are single-arg (paired with substring slicing for from-position semantics) → PASS

falsifiers_preregistered.condition: empty-source guard hypothesis — helper detects file resolution failure (cwd not at hive root) with eprintln
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: static read of empty-source guard branch + zero-total guard branch
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: both guards (body == '' AND total == 0) present with clear eprintln → PASS

data.invocation_site: _cmd_selftest after _selftest_check_parity() and before final summary println
data.summary_text_post_change: ok (10 shape checks PASS + dispatch/selftest parity + label/body alignment — full matrix coverage)
data.exec_evidence: selftest stdout includes 'PASS label-body — 10 lines aligned (label NN == body NN)' between parity line and final summary

axis_b.name: drift detection scope expansion (what is now caught vs not)

newly_caught_drift_classes: _assert_shape("C5 raw75", _check_raw87()) — label says 75, body calls _check_raw87 → exit 5
any swap of body fn within an existing _assert_shape line (90% of intra-line edit errors)
still_NOT_caught: _assert_shape("C5 raw75", _check_raw75()) where _check_raw75 has been semantically rewritten to do something unrelated to raw 75 (rename without renaming the fn)
missing whole _assert_shape line is caught by parity (O5), not by alignment
data.interpretation: alignment closes the within-line semantic-drift sub-class; combined with parity (count-drift) the selftest now defends against both forgot-to-mirror AND within-line-mismatch failure modes

data.observed_stderr: raw_registration_gate_lint: selftest FAIL label-body — line 550 label=75 body=71
data.observed_stdout_truncation: 10 PASS shape lines + PASS parity line all printed before the FAIL — confirms the helper runs after parity and stops at the first violation in the offending line via violators++ + final exit 5
data.revert_verification: after reverting to _check_raw75, full selftest emits 'PASS label-body — 10 lines aligned' and 'ok (... + label/body alignment — full matrix coverage)' with exit 0
data.evidence_class: live source-edit cycle (genuine FAIL-path proof, not fabricated negative test)

axis_d.name: string parsing approach (single-arg index_of + substring slicing)

data.approach: for each candidate line: index_of(' raw') → substring tail → tail.index_of('"') → label substring; index_of('_check_raw') → substring tail → tail.index_of('(') → body substring; compare strings
data.rationale_vs_2arg_index_of: existing tool uses only single-arg index_of (lines 139, 448, 609); 2-arg form unverified in this hexa dialect. Substring slicing is established (line 191, _read_file uses _shell_escape; lines 588-591 in argv parsing slice by index)
data.rationale_vs_regex_extract: hexa stdlib does not expose regex match groups; full grep-with-capture would require gawk (BSD awk on Mac doesn't support); pure-hexa is portable across the runtime
edge_case_robustness: leading whitespace !=4 → starts_with('    _assert_shape("C') guard skips line entirely
missing label number → empty label_num strings; ('' != body_num) triggers FAIL-path
missing body fn → empty body_num strings; same
comment lines containing _assert_shape → first guard '    _assert_shape("C' rules out (comments don't start with that exact prefix)

fan_out.correlation_method: axis_a (implementation) vs axis_b (drift scope) ~0.30; axis_a vs axis_c (live evidence) ~0.40 (evidence comes from running implementation); axis_a vs axis_d (parsing approach) ~0.50 (parsing is part of implementation); axis_b vs axis_c ~0.10; axis_b vs axis_d ~0.05; axis_c vs axis_d ~0.10. Pairwise mean ≈ 0.24. Note: axis_a-vs-axis_d slightly above mid; both anchored to helper body; rest are well-spread.

axes_surfaced.name: verification mechanism — parse-own-source vs runtime-introspection

options_considered.label: parse own source via _read_file + line iteration + substring extraction
options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected — minimal new infrastructure (reuses _read_file), explicit + readable, deterministic

options_considered.label: runtime introspection of fn name when calling each _assert_shape (require body to also pass an fn-name string)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: duplicates information — caller would need to write _assert_shape("C5 raw75", "_check_raw75", _check_raw75()) — verbose and still trusts the human to keep the string in sync with the actual call

options_considered.label: macro/code-gen — generate the 10 _assert_shape calls from a single declaration list
options_considered.rejected_rationale: hexa dialect does not expose a macro system; would need an external code generator

options_considered.label: skip alignment, accept O6 as low-likelihood manual hygiene
options_considered.rejected_rationale: rejected — predecessor cycle explicitly named O6 as the next step; defaulting to skip leaks the residual

axes_surfaced.rationale: A is minimal-blast and deterministic. The helper runs in O(N) over source lines, parses 10 lines, exits cleanly. No new dependencies, no caller-side noise.

axes_surfaced.name: negative-path verification — synthetic vs live

options_considered.label: synthetic test — pass an in-memory mismatch fixture
options_considered.rejected_rationale: would require additional test infrastructure (mock _read_file or test-only fn argument); over-engineered

options_considered.label: live source-edit cycle — temporarily mismatch a real _assert_shape line, run selftest, observe FAIL, revert
options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected — same approach as predecessor cycle's inadvertent F3 evidence, but this time deliberate; produces genuine FAIL-path proof in <30 seconds

options_considered.label: skip negative-path verification — trust the implementation reads correctly
options_considered.rejected_rationale: rejected — raw 71 falsifier mandate requires actual verdict evidence per falsifier; trust is not evidence

options_considered.label: two test fixtures (one matching, one mismatched) shipped alongside the helper
options_considered.rejected_rationale: ships test data into production binary; clutters distribution; live edit-revert is cleaner

axes_surfaced.rationale: Live edit-revert produces real FAIL-path evidence (rc=5 + line-number + label=75 body=71 diagnostic) without persistent test infrastructure. The revert is a single Edit operation. Same pattern as the previous wave's F3 evidence (which was inadvertent); this wave makes it deliberate.

axes_surfaced.name: scope creep boundary

options_considered.label: also enforce delegated-tool invocation arguments match (e.g., raw 33 lint always called with --severity block)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: out of scope — different layer; defer as O7 if it ever surfaces

options_considered.label: also add a CI runner that re-runs selftest on every commit
options_considered.rejected_rationale: out of scope — harness-level concern requiring user confirmation for hooks/CI config

options_considered.label: scope strictly to label-body alignment within _assert_shape lines (close O6 cleanly)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected — predecessor obstruction closed, no new sub-residual surfaced unless a new failure class is observed

options_considered.label: also extend alignment to non-shape-assertion call sites in _cmd_selftest
options_considered.rejected_rationale: no other _check_rawNN call sites in _cmd_selftest besides the 10 _assert_shape lines; nothing to align beyond

axes_surfaced.rationale: One obstruction in (O6), one obstruction out, no new residual unless evidence emerges. Compounding via small disciplined cycles.

tier_1_promotions.title: label-body alignment OPERATIONAL — _selftest_check_label_body helper runs every selftest
tier_1_promotions.claim: _cmd_selftest now invokes _selftest_check_label_body() after the parity check. Helper parses own source, extracts (label NN, body MM) from each _assert_shape("CN rawNN", _check_rawMM()) line, asserts NN == MM, exits 5 on first violation. F1+F2 PASS — selftest stdout shows 'PASS label-body — 10 lines aligned (label NN == body NN)'.

tier_1_promotions.title: Live negative-path PROVEN via deliberate edit-FAIL-revert cycle — not fabricated
tier_1_promotions.claim: F3 PASS evidence: deliberate _check_raw75 → _check_raw71 swap on line 550 produced rc=5 + 'selftest FAIL label-body — line 550 label=75 body=71' diagnostic. After revert, selftest restored to clean PASS. Helper would catch a real-world drift exactly the same way.

tier_1_promotions.title: Zero regression on production paths — F4 PASS
tier_1_promotions.claim: After adding the alignment helper, hexa tool/raw_registration_gate_lint.hexa --report --target-rule raw:97 still emits fail_count=0, checks_total=10, verdict=ok. The helper is strictly selftest-local.

fixpoint_marker: O6-closure-converged: predecessor obstruction O6 (label-body alignment unchecked) has CLOSED via _selftest_check_label_body helper. Live negative evidence (F3) deliberately reproduced exit-5 + diagnostic, then cleanly reverted. NO new sub-residual surfaced — selftest now defends against (a) shape regressions (10/10 contracts), (b) forgot-to-mirror count drift (parity), and (c) within-line label-body misalignment. Predecessor open obstructions O2/O3/O4 remain (require user confirmation: harness wiring, launchd, classifier-version bump). Loop CAN compound further on user-confirmation-required surfaces only with explicit greenlight.
